title: athlete — TenancyHook + lifecycle hooks
description: Cross-module hook implementations. Athlete does not register a bootstrapper (nothing to hydrate at tenant-boot). Registers four cascade hooks: branch-archive prevention, user-archive prevention, tenant-erasure cascade, and age-group snapshot resolution on athlete creation.

hooks.name: PreventAthleteOrphansOnBranchArchived
hooks.purpose: Refuse the archive when any Athlete row referencing the branch still has status IN ('active', 'paused'). Distinct from the FK RESTRICT (which only blocks hard-delete) — this blocks the archive transition because athletes need a target branch to continue existing. Graduated + withdrawn + archived athletes DO NOT block (they're terminal states + the audit trail preserves the branch reference).
hooks.returns: BRANCH_HAS_ACTIVE_ATHLETES (409) with the athlete count in the error payload
hooks.note: Priority 30 — runs BEFORE PreventStaffOrphansOnBranchArchived (priority higher — coach + athlete depend on the same branch) so the athlete-count message wins when both would fire. HR usually addresses staff first, then athletes.

hooks.name: PreventUserArchiveWithActiveAthlete
hooks.purpose: Refuse the User soft-delete when the User has an active (deleted_at IS NULL) linked Athlete row (user_id-based FK) OR when the User is consent_recorded_by_user_id for any active minor athlete. The archive flow must run first — Athlete.status → archived — which fires AthleteArchived → UnblockUserArchiveWhenLastAthleteArchived lifts the block. For the consent recorder case: guardian handoff to another User must complete first (a minor cannot lose their guardian without a replacement recorded).
hooks.returns: USER_HAS_ACTIVE_ATHLETE (409) with the athlete_id + tenant_id in the error payload; USER_IS_MINOR_CONSENT_RECORDER (409) for the guardian-handoff scenario
hooks.note: Priority 45 — runs AFTER PreventUserArchiveWithActiveStaff (priority 40) — staff record wins when both would fire because employment offboarding is the load-bearing first step; athlete archive typically follows staff offboarding (a coach leaves → their athletes reassign).

hooks.name: PurgeAthleteDataForErasedTenant
hooks.purpose: Cascade delete via FK; writes compliance audit trail entry + emits count metrics per entity family (athletes purged, medical_history rows migrated to compliance archive, guardian rows cascade-purged via athlete-guardian module). Audit rows outlive the source rows per financial + medical retention (medical_history in particular — retained 7 years even after the tenant is erased, per legal requirement).
hooks.compliance: GDPR Art. 17 (right to erasure) + medical-retention exception. Medical history rows are NOT purged with the tenant — they migrate to a compliance-archive table (retained in encrypted form by ops for the 7-year window, keyed to a tenant-anonymised id). Same pattern as staff_compensation_history in the staff module.

hooks.name: SetAgeGroupSnapshotOnAthleteCreated
hooks.purpose: In-band resolution of Athlete.current_age_group_id at create time. Runs AgeGroupResolver::resolveFor(athlete.dob, sport_key=null, on_date=today) + updates the row before returning the create response. Without it, athletes would appear age-group-less until the next nightly RollAthleteAgeGroupSnapshotJob pass — breaks the enrollment-form flow because the age-group filter can't match.
hooks.note: Priority 20 — runs BEFORE notification listeners (priority 100 default) so the notification renderer sees the resolved age group in the payload.

no_bootstrapper.reason: Athlete does not need a tenant-boot hydration path — every row loads on demand from the DB. The AthleteRepository + AgeGroupSnapshotResolver bindings cache lazily on first query.
